title: [Bug]: [Bug]: Cross-page table highlights only show on the first page with MinerU backend when clicking a chunk in the preview.
author: one985
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

16283

### RAGFlow image version

0.26.1

### Other environment information

```Markdown
Using MinerU as the OCR backend.
```

### Actual behavior

When clicking a chunk that spans multiple PDF pages (e.g., a cross-page table on page 7 and page 8), the PDF preview on the left only highlights the first page. The subsequent page's text/table rows are not highlighted at all.

I have traced the entire data flow (Frontend -> RAGFlow Backend -> MinerU -> RAGFlow Backend -> Frontend) to debug this issue. The root cause is located in the RAGFlow backend's processing of the data provided by MinerU. MinerU outputs complete coordinates for all pages of the cross-page table in its raw `_middle.json` file, but the RAGFlow backend only extracts the coordinates of the first page when generating the chunk data. As a result, the frontend receives an incomplete `positions` array, which causes the highlights on the subsequent pages to be entirely missing.

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
1. Upload a PDF file that contains a table spanning multiple pages.
2. Use MinerU as the OCR backend for parsing the document.
3. Go to the "Parsed Chunk" preview page (`/chunk/parsed`).
4. Find a chunk that contains the cross-page table.
5. Click on this chunk in the right sidebar.
6. Observe that only the first page's table content is highlighted in the left PDF viewer, while the rest of the pages are not highlighted.
```

### Additional information

_No response_

title: Admin server surfaces DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL via 'list envs' but init_default_admin() hardcodes the account
author: kobihikri
body:
### Summary

The admin server surfaces `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL` as if it configures the bootstrap admin account
(it shows up in `list envs` and in `services.py`), but `init_default_admin()` hardcodes the email and
password, so setting `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL` (or `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD`) doesn't change the
account that actually gets created.

To be clear up front: I know the `admin@ragflow.io` / `admin` default is **documented** and that the
CLI can change the password after first login — so this isn't a "hidden default credentials" report.
It's a smaller consistency point: one of these env vars *looks* effective but isn't wired to the
bootstrap.

### Observation

`admin/server/services.py` lists `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL` among the environments the admin service
reports ([`services.py:440`](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/blob/e54e7ec7ef6c42b89e966473e7a2d96296997629/admin/server/services.py#L440)):

```python
env_kv = {"env": "DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL", "value": os.getenv("DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL", "admin@ragflow.io")}
```

But the account is actually created by `init_default_admin()`
([`admin/server/auth.py:88-100`](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/blob/e54e7ec7ef6c42b89e966473e7a2d96296997629/admin/server/auth.py#L88-L100)), which hardcodes both fields:

```python
default_admin = {
    "password": encode_to_base64("admin"),
    "email": "admin@ragflow.io",
    ...
}
```

`check_admin()` (same file) hardcodes the same pair. Neither reads `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL` /
`DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD`. (A separate, opt-in path, `api/db/init_data.py:init_superuser`, *does*
read `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD`, but the admin server doesn't use it.)

### Effect (inference)

An operator who sets `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL=me@corp.com` before first boot will see that value
echoed by `list envs`, but the admin account that gets created is still `admin@ragflow.io` — the env
var reads as effective while the behaviour is fixed. `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD` is similarly not
honoured by this path.

### Possible directions (your call)

- Have `init_default_admin()` / `check_admin()` read `os.getenv("DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL", "admin@ragflow.io")`
  and `os.getenv("DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD", "admin")`, matching what `list envs` implies, **or**
- Drop `DEFAULT_SUPERUSER_EMAIL` from the surfaced env list so it doesn't read as effective when the
  bootstrap is fixed.

Happy to open a small PR either way. Thanks for RAGFlow — the admin CLI is a nice touch.

<sub>AI-assisted; I reproduced the code paths and the `list envs` doc example myself against commit `e54e7ec7ef6c`.</sub>

title: [Feature Request]: Pluggable GraphRAG extractor interface
author: gotoin
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
RAGFlow currently provides built-in GraphRAG extraction methods, including `light`, `general`, and `ner`. These work for common document-centric cases, but adding a domain-specific extractor appears to require changing RAGFlow source code and the hardcoded method list.

This is a problem for:

- code repositories, where entities and relations can come from AST/static analysis;
- enterprise metadata and lineage, where entities come from existing catalogs;
- low-resource deployments, where users may prefer non-LLM or small-model extraction;
- domain-specific extraction, such as medical, legal, finance, source code, and logs.
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

Introduce a pluggable GraphRAG extractor interface.

Existing extractors can remain registered as built-ins:

- `light`
- `general`
- `ner`

Custom extractors could be provided through configuration or API, for example:

- Python class path;
- external HTTP endpoint;
- plugin registry, if RAGFlow already has or plans one.

The custom extractor should return normalized entities and relations compatible with the existing downstream GraphRAG pipeline, so RAGFlow can continue to handle graph merge, entity/relation embedding, Elasticsearch/Infinity storage, knowledge graph retrieval, and `Use knowledge graph`.

### Describe implementation you've considered

One possible implementation is an extractor registry, with the current extractors registered as defaults:

```python
register_graphrag_extractor("light", LightKGExt)
register_graphrag_extractor("general", GeneralKGExt)
register_graphrag_extractor("ner", NerKGExt)
```

Then the current method selection logic can resolve extractors from the registry.

For out-of-process extractors, RAGFlow could optionally support an HTTP provider:

```yaml
graphrag:
  method: external
  extractor_url: http://extractor:8080/extract
```

Example request:

```json
{
  "doc_id": "xxx",
  "chunks": ["..."],
  "language": "English",
  "entity_types": ["service", "api", "table", "config"]
}
```

Example response:

```json
{
  "entities": [],
  "relations": []
}
```

Backward compatibility should remain unchanged, for example existing datasets using `method: light` should continue to work without configuration changes.

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown
Potential use cases:

1. Code repositories: use a static analyzer to extract packages, files, classes, functions, calls, imports, and ownership.
2. Enterprise metadata: import tables, jobs, APIs, owners, and data lineage from an existing metadata system.
3. Low-resource deployments: use non-LLM or small-model extractors for entity/relation extraction, while still using RAGFlow for graph storage and retrieval.
4. Domain-specific knowledge bases: medical, legal, finance, logs, source code, and other domains with specialized entity/relation schemas.
```

### Additional information

Questions for maintainers:

- Would maintainers prefer a Python-class registry, an HTTP extractor interface, or both?
- Should custom extractors be exposed in the UI, or only through API/config first?
- Is there an existing plugin mechanism intended for dataset parsing or GraphRAG extraction?

title: [Bug]: Google OAuth popup title shows "Google-google-drive" instead of "Google Drive"
author: Harsh23Kashyap
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

b0cac0ac (current `main`)

### RAGFlow image version

`main` source; reference v0.26.4

### Other environment information

- Python 3.13 backend
- Google OAuth web flow at `POST /connectors/google/oauth/web/{start,callback}` (per `common.data_source.google_util.constant`)
- Tested with `google-drive` and `gmail` source types

### What happened?

When a user authorizes Google Drive (or Gmail) via the Web OAuth flow, the popup window that confirms authorization displays the title `Google-google-drive Authorization` (with a literal hyphen and a literal `-drive` after the `Google-`).

The bug is in `_render_web_oauth_popup` at [`api/apps/restful_apis/connector_api.py:352-353`](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/blob/b0cac0ac/api/apps/restful_apis/connector_api.py):

```python
html = WEB_OAUTH_POPUP_TEMPLATE.format(
    title=f"Google {source.capitalize()} Authorization",
    ...
)
```

`source` is one of `"google-drive"` or `"gmail"` (validated in the route handler at `start_google_web_oauth` at line 368: `if source not in ("google-drive", "gmail")`). `str.capitalize()` lower-cases the rest of the string and upper-cases only the first character:

- `"google-drive".capitalize()` → `"Google-drive"` (the `-drive` suffix survives unchanged)
- `"gmail".capitalize()` → `"Gmail"` (correct)

The TODO comment in the file (`# TODO(google-oauth): title/heading/message may need to reflect drive/gmail based on cached type`) already flags this as an outstanding concern, but the current implementation produces a visibly broken title.

### Actual behavior

The OAuth popup title is `Google-google-drive Authorization` (literal lowercase `-drive` after a hyphen). Users who have multiple Google tabs open (e.g. authorizing Drive in one tab and Gmail in another) will see two popups with non-distinct, malformed titles. Worse, a user who sees `Google-google-drive` may suspect phishing because no legitimate Google product is named that way.

### Expected behavior

The title should read `Google Drive Authorization` (or `Gmail Authorization`) — proper product names, no hyphens leaking through.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up a Google OAuth web app credential as documented in the connector guide.
2. As an authenticated user, navigate to the data-source Google Drive connector.
3. Click "Authorize with Google" — the OAuth flow opens a popup window.
4. Inspect the popup window's `<title>` and `<h1>` — observe `Google-google-drive Authorization` instead of `Google Drive Authorization`.

Or, in a unit test, build the same `f"Google {source.capitalize()} Authorization"` string:

```python
>>> f"Google {'google-drive'.capitalize()} Authorization"
'Google-google-drive Authorization'   # <-- wrong
>>> f"Google {'gmail'.capitalize()} Authorization"
'Google Gmail Authorization'             # <-- right
```

The asymmetry confirms the bug.

### Actual vs expected

- `source = "google-drive"` → actual `Google-google-drive Authorization`, expected `Google Drive Authorization`.
- `source = "gmail"` → actual `Google Gmail Authorization`, expected `Gmail Authorization` (single-word product needs no `Google` prefix; matches what users see at https://accounts.google.com/...).

The fix is to use a proper product-name map rather than `str.capitalize()`. The TODO already suggests this approach ("title/heading/message may need to reflect drive/gmail based on cached type").

title: [Bug]: Parsing status counts are null when `include_parsing_status=true`  in API 'list datasets'
author: Aeovy
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.

- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

/

### RAGFlow image version

0.26.3

### Other environment information

```Markdown

```

### Actual behavior

## Parsing status counts are null when `include_parsing_status=true`

### Actual behavior

When calling the **List Datasets API** with `include_parsing_status=true`, all parsing status count fields are returned as `null`:

- `running_count`
- `unstarted_count`
- `done_count`
- `failed_count`


### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Call the List Datasets API.
2. Set the query parameter `include_parsing_status=true`.
3. Check the parsing status count fields in the response.

### Expected behavior

The parsing status count fields should return valid integer values, including `0` when no datasets match a particular status, instead of `null`.

### Actual response
```json
{
  "running_count": null,
  "unstarted_count": null,
  "done_count": null,
  "failed_count": null
}
```
### Additional information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Video descriptions are duplicated before tokenization
author: 77zane
body:
### Self-checks

- [x] I searched existing issues and pull requests for duplicated video descriptions.
- [x] The issue reproduces on the current `main` branch.

### Describe the bug

The video path in `rag/app/picture.py` duplicates the vision model response immediately before tokenization:

```python
ans += "\n" + ans
tokenize(doc, ans, ...)
```

If the vision model returns `A concise video description.`, RAGFlow indexes:

```text
A concise video description.
A concise video description.
```

The statement was introduced with the original Gemini video parsing support in #10671. That PR contains no design requirement for duplicating or weighting the generated text. The adjacent image path combines two different sources (`OCR text + vision description`), which indicates the self-concatenation in the video path is a copy/paste error.

### Impact

Every successfully parsed video stores duplicated textual content. This unnecessarily repeats search text, tokenization output, and embedding input, and can distort chunk content and retrieval scoring.

### Expected behavior

The vision model response should be tokenized exactly once.

### Minimal fix

Remove the self-concatenation and add a regression test that asserts a single model response is passed once to `tokenize`.

title: [Feature Request]: Support Fun-ASR-Flash in the Tongyi-Qianwen speech-to-text provider
author: 77zane
body:
### Self-checks

- [x] I have searched for similar issues and found no existing request for Fun-ASR-Flash in the Tongyi-Qianwen provider.
- [x] This request describes a focused, testable provider enhancement.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

RAGFlow's `QWenSeq2txt` adapter currently calls `dashscope.MultiModalConversation` with the legacy `audio` message shape. Alibaba Cloud's hosted Fun-ASR-Flash snapshots, such as `fun-asr-flash-2026-06-15`, use the workspace-native synchronous HTTP endpoint instead:

`/api/v1/services/aigc/multimodal-generation/generation`

The request uses `input_audio` and audio format parameters. Consequently, selecting a Fun-ASR-Flash model in the Tongyi-Qianwen speech-to-text provider cannot work through the existing path.

This is separate from #15449, which concerns a self-hosted FunASR OpenAI-compatible service rather than Alibaba Cloud's hosted Fun-ASR-Flash API.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Extend `QWenSeq2txt` with a narrowly scoped path for model names beginning with `fun-asr-flash`:

- preserve the existing behavior for all other Qwen audio models;
- convert local audio to a data URI while continuing to accept URL/data-URI inputs;
- derive the native `/api/v1` base from a workspace compatible-mode URL;
- call the documented multimodal-generation endpoint with `input_audio` and the required `format`;
- parse `output.text` and preserve RAGFlow's streaming interface;
- register the current hosted snapshot in `llm_factories.json`.

### Validation

The proposed implementation has been validated with:

- an official WAV sample through the hosted API;
- a real MP3 uploaded and indexed through RAGFlow;
- unit tests for the native request, the unchanged legacy Qwen path, and streaming behavior;
- Ruff checks and JSON validation.

### Documentation

- https://help.aliyun.com/document_detail/2979031.html
- https://help.aliyun.com/document_detail/2869541.html
### Why a dedicated adapter path is necessary (official evidence)

Alibaba Cloud's [Fun-ASR RESTful API reference](https://help.aliyun.com/en/model-studio/fun-asr-recorded-speech-recognition-http-api) makes the incompatibilities with RAGFlow's existing Qwen audio path explicit:

| Adapter change | Official API requirement | Why the existing path is insufficient |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Call the workspace-native HTTP endpoint | The Fun-ASR-Flash synchronous section states that SDK calls are not supported and specifies `POST /api/v1/services/aigc/multimodal-generation/generation`. | The existing adapter calls `dashscope.MultiModalConversation`, so a direct HTTP path is required. |
| Use the `input_audio` message shape | `input.messages`, `content`, `type: input_audio`, `input_audio`, and `input_audio.data` are documented as required for an audio request. | The existing Qwen path sends the legacy `audio` content shape, which does not match this API contract. |
| Send `parameters.format` | The request schema marks `parameters` and `format` as **Required**, and says the value must match the actual audio format. | The legacy request has no Fun-ASR-Flash `parameters.format` field, so the adapter must derive and send it. |
| Encode local files as Data URIs | `input_audio.data` accepts either a public URL or a Base64 Data URI; the reference gives the exact `data:{MIME_TYPE};base64,...` form. | RAGFlow supplies local file paths, which the remote API cannot read directly. |
| Parse `output.text` | The documented non-streaming response returns the accumulated transcription in `output.text`. | The legacy Qwen response parser reads `output.choices[].message.content`, so a separate response parser is required. |

`sample_rate` is documented as **Optional**. The implementation omits it instead of declaring a fixed value that may not match remote or compressed audio.

The [official speech-to-text model list](https://help.aliyun.com/en/model-studio/asr-model/) separately confirms that `fun-asr-flash-2026-06-15` is an offline HTTP model with a five-minute audio limit.

title: [Feature Request]: Support date filter and secondary sorting based on date extracted from document content
author: lmz1996
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

```Markdown
Scenario:
Our knowledge base stores a large quantity of papers and technical reports.
During retrieval, users want to obtain latest information and automatically filter out outdated documents.
Pain point:
In many cases, we cannot get reliable modified_time from source document platform API.
We expect RAGFlow to natively identify date information within document content (such as paper publication date), **without users writing extra scripts or custom code**.
Expected retrieval logic:
1. Primary ranking: vector similarity
2. Secondary rule: Results with similar similarity will be sorted by document internal date (newest first)
## Questions to confirm
1. Does the current version of RAGFlow have built-in capability to extract dates from document text for time filtering and sorting? No extra custom code needed.
2. If this feature is not available now, is there a relevant development plan on the roadmap
```

### Describe the feature you'd like

I hope RAGFlow can realize the following capabilities out-of-box:
Automatically extract date information inside documents during parsing phase, and support:
1. Time range filtering when retrieving chunks, to eliminate outdated content;
2. Secondary chronological sorting for records with close similarity scores.


The whole workflow should be available directly in RAGFlow UI or API.
Users are not required to develop additional scripts for date extraction and metadata injection.


### Describe implementation you've considered

_No response_

### Documentation, adoption, use case

```Markdown

```

### Additional information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: validate_immutable_fields / validateImmutableDocumentFields allow overwriting run and progress_msg
author: Harsh23Kashyap
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

891261e1 (current upstream `main`)

### RAGFlow image version

`main` source; reference v0.26.4

### Other environment information

- Python backend (Flask + Pydantic v2 + GORM-equivalent via peewee)
- Go backend (Gin + GORM, currently mid-migration)
- Single deployment under test
- Document engine: Elasticsearch 8.11.3 (docker)

### What happened?

`validate_immutable_fields` in [`api/utils/validation_utils.py:1059`](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/blob/891261e1/api/utils/validation_utils.py#L1059) and the new `validateImmutableDocumentFields` in [`internal/service/document.go`](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/blob/891261e1/internal/service/document.go) (just shipped in PR #16836) both reject attempts to overwrite `chunk_count` / `token_count` / `progress` via `PATCH /api/v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/documents/{document_id}` (Python) and `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id` (Go).

The original issue **#16831** explicitly listed five ingestion-managed fields: `progress`, `run`, `progress_msg`, `chunk_num`, `token_num`. The fix scoped to "parity with Python" — but Python's `validate_immutable_fields` was also incomplete. An external caller can still overwrite two of the five fields:

```http
PUT /api/v1/documents/{document_id}
Content-Type: application/json

{"run": "0"}
```

```http
PUT /api/v1/documents/{document_id}
Content-Type: application/json

{"progress_msg": "fake"}
```

Both calls succeed (200 OK). The DB row is updated immediately via GORM full-record upsert (`documentDAO.Update`) or the Python PATCH handler. The ingestion pipeline's `ProgressSink` (Go) and the docStateUpdater (Python) will later overwrite the new values, creating a transient inconsistency window during which an external caller has successfully corrupted observable state.

### Actual behavior

Both `run` and `progress_msg` are accepted as overwrite values. The DB row is mutated. The transient window is small but observable to any UI consumer that refreshes between the call and the next pipeline tick.

### Expected behavior

`run` and `progress_msg` should be rejected with `CodeDataError` (Go) / `RetCode.DATA_ERROR` (Python), matching the existing `chunk_count` / `token_count` / `progress` behaviour. The pattern is already established in both validators; this is purely a coverage extension.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Pick any document that has been through the pipeline (so `run` is non-empty and `progress_msg` was written by `ProgressSink`).
2. Send `PUT /api/v1/documents/{document_id}` with `{"run": "0"}` (or `progress_msg: "fake"`). Auth required, but no role gate beyond authenticated user.
3. Observe the response: 200 OK, value updated.
4. Repeat with `{"chunk_count": 0}` and confirm the contrasting rejection (`Can't change 'chunk_count'.`, 4xx).

### Actual vs expected

- `chunk_count` overwrite: rejected with `Can't change 'chunk_count'.` (correct).
- `run` overwrite: accepted with 200 OK (incorrect — should be rejected).
- `progress_msg` overwrite: accepted with 200 OK (incorrect — should be rejected).

The Go PUT endpoint at `internal/service/document.go:598` already calls `validateImmutableDocumentFields` (per #16836); that helper only checks `ChunkNum` / `TokenNum` / `Progress`. The Python PATCH handler at `api/apps/services/document_service.py` calls `validate_immutable_fields(req, doc)`; that helper only checks `chunk_count` / `token_count` / `progress`. Both need the additional `run` and `progress_msg` checks.

The original issue #16831 is a related predecessor; PR #16836 covered the parity-with-Python half of the fix.

title: [Internal Discussion] Ingestion pipeline: hardcoded 60s timeouts cause false cancellations for large documents
author: xugangqiang
body:
> **Note:** This issue is for internal discussion among the RAGFlow team. External contributors, please do NOT submit PRs for this issue. Thank you!

## Summary

Two separate hardcoded 60-second timeouts in the Go ingestion pipeline cause tasks processing large documents (tens of MB PDFs) to be falsely reported as "cancelled" without user action. The root causes span the pipeline framework, the Tokenizer component, and the embedder call chain.

## Symptoms

1. **Pipeline-level 60s timeout** (already fixed locally): `pipeline.go` injected `WithComponentTimeoutOverride(ctx, 60s)`, overriding per-class defaults (600s). Removed in local branch `feat/refactor-and-nats`.

2. **Tokenizer 60s timeout** (still present): `tokenizer.go:114` — `encodeWithTimeout` wraps each batch embedding call with `runtime.WithTimeout(ctx, 60s)`. Large documents with many chunks exceed 60s even for a single batch.

3. **Timeout vs. cancellation conflation**: `runTask` in `ingestion_service.go` treats `context.DeadlineExceeded` (component timeout) the same as `context.Canceled` (user cancellation). Both result in STOPPED status with "Task stopped by user." — misleading for debugging.

## Root Cause Analysis

### Python vs Go timeout behavior

| Aspect | Python (production default) | Go (current) |
|---|---|---|
| Pipeline-level timeout | None | 60s (removed in local branch) |
| Tokenizer @timeout(60) / tokenizerTimeout | No-op unless ENABLE_TIMEOUT_ASSERTION is set | Always enforced |
| Component timeout default | 600s via @timeout(10*60) | 600s via defaultComponentTimeout (but was overridden by 60s pipeline value) |
| Context propagation to embedder | N/A (sync @timeout is a watchdog) | Context NOT passed — embedder uses context.Background() |

### The embedder context problem

`encodeWithTimeout` creates a timeout context but never passes it to `embedder.Encode()`:
```go
// tokenizer.go:466-479
func encodeWithTimeout(ctx context.Context, embedder Embedder, texts []string) ([]EmbeddingResult, error) {
    timeoutErr := runtime.WithTimeout(ctx, tokenizerTimeout, func(timeoutCtx context.Context) error {
        results, encErr = embedder.Encode(texts) // timeoutCtx NOT used!
        return encErr
    })
```

The `Embedder` interface (`Encode(texts []string)`) has no context parameter. The model driver (`Embed(...)`) creates its own `context.Background()`. This means:
- `runtime.WithTimeout` acts as a **watchdog** — it lets the embedder call complete, then discards the result if the deadline expired
- A successful embedding call taking >60s is **wasted** — results are thrown away

The correct design: pass `ctx` through `Embedder.Encode(ctx, texts)` → `ModelDriver.Embed(ctx, ...)` → `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, ...)`, so timeouts cancel in-flight HTTP requests rather than discarding completed results.

## Proposed Fixes

### Phase 1 (immediate — in current branch)
1. ✅ Remove pipeline `WithComponentTimeoutOverride` injection (done)
2. Distinguish `DeadlineExceeded` → FAILED from `Canceled` → STOPPED in `runTask` (done)
3. Make `tokenizerTimeout` configurable via `COMPONENT_EXEC_TIMEOUT_TOKENIZER`, raise default to 600s

### Phase 2 (design discussion needed)
4. Add `ctx context.Context` to `Embedder.Encode()` interface and all implementations
5. Add `ctx context.Context` to `ModelDriver.Embed()` interface and all ~20 model drivers
6. Remove `encodeWithTimeout` watchdog — rely on context propagation for proper cancellation

## Affected Files

### Already modified
- `internal/ingestion/pipeline/pipeline.go` — removed `stageTimeout()` and `WithComponentTimeoutOverride` injection
- `internal/ingestion/service/ingestion_service.go` — split `DeadlineExceeded` / `Canceled` error handling, added `markTimeoutProgress`

### To be modified (Phase 1)
- `internal/ingestion/component/tokenizer.go` — `tokenizerTimeout` configurable

### To be modified (Phase 2)
- `internal/ingestion/component/tokenizer.go` — `Embedder` interface, `encodeWithTimeout`
- `internal/ingestion/task/embedder.go` — `embedder.Encode` implementation
- `internal/entity/models/*.go` — ~20 `Embed()` method signatures
- `internal/service/citation.go` — embedder usage

## Related
- Branch: `feat/refactor-and-nats`
- Python reference: `rag/flow/tokenizer/tokenizer.py:99` (`@timeout(60)`), `common/connection_utils.py:52` (`ENABLE_TIMEOUT_ASSERTION` gate)

title: [Bug]: Flow handoff overwrites image parser output format from JSON to text
author: pramudyalyza
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

8f0632c

### RAGFlow image version

v0.25.6

### Other environment information

```Markdown

```

### Actual behavior

In a custom ingestion pipeline, an image Parser output contains a non-empty `json` field with `doc_type_kwd = image`, but the data received by Token Chunker has `output_format = "text"`

Because of this, Token Chunker follows the text branch, reads the empty top level `text` field, and returns empty chunks

Raw Token Chunker input example:

```JSON
{
  "html": "",
  "json": [
    {
      "text": "田\nE\nSchedule Trigger\nGet Texono\nFetch ltem\nSplit Item\nIF Not Empty\nAggregatl\nSet Data2\nExecute Sub Workflon\n(Deactivated)\nGET:1\nipc/lapltesi.alge\nrkllow: KYUTVIG1PURVP.",
      "doc_type_kwd": "image",
      "img_id": "imagetemps-83f14d867a9e11f182b1f58af7b85a6b"
    }
  ],
  "markdown": "",
  "text": "",
  "name": "Screenshot 2026-07-05 175128.png",
  "output_format": "text"
}
```

The final Token Chunker result is:

```JSON
{
  "chunks": []
}
```

### Expected behavior

If the Parser produces a non-empty `json` result for an image, and the JSON item has `doc_type_kwd = image` with non-empty text, the Token Chunker should process the image Parser JSON result and generate chunks instead of treating the input as empty text

Expected handoff behavior:

```JSON
{
  "json": [
    {
      "text": "...",
      "doc_type_kwd": "image"
    }
  ],
  "text": "",
  "output_format": "json"
}
```

Expected Token Chunker output:

```JSON
{
  "chunks": [...]
}
```

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
1. Use RAGFlow v0.25.6 with a custom ingestion pipeline
2. Create a minimal pipeline:

   → Parser
   → Token Chunker
   → Indexer

3. Add temporary logging before TokenChunkerFromUpstream.model_validate(kwargs) in:

   /ragflow/rag/flow/chunker/token_chunker.py

   Example:

   print("TOKEN_CHUNKER_RAW_KWARGS:", kwargs, flush=True)

4. Run an image through the pipeline
5. Observe that Token Chunker receives:
   - non-empty `json`
   - `json[0].doc_type_kwd = image`
   - non-empty `json[0].text`
   - empty top-level `text`
   - but `output_format = "text"`

6. Observe that Token Chunker returns empty chunks
7. As a confirmation test only, temporarily force:

   kwargs["output_format"] = "json"
   from_upstream = TokenChunkerFromUpstream.model_validate(kwargs)

8. Run the same image again
9. Image chunking works after forcing `output_format = "json"`, which indicates the issue is in the flow handoff / output format mapping, not OCR/VLM parsing or JSON chunk construction
```

### Additional information

Related discussion:
https://github.com/orgs/infiniflow/discussions/16648

title: [INTERNAL DISCUSSION — do not work on] Remove unused componentIndexMap and ProgressEvent.Index/Total from ingestion pipeline
author: xugangqiang
body:
> **⚠️ INTERNAL DISCUSSION ONLY — external contributors please do NOT work on this issue.**

## Context

`pipeline.componentIndexMap()` generates a 0-based index for each canvas component by sorting cpnIDs alphabetically. This index flows through `ProgressEvent.Index` → `progressSink.OnComponentProgress` → `ingestion_task_log.component_index` column.

## Finding

**The `component_index` field is write-only.** A full codebase search shows:
- DAO layer: zero reads (`AggregateProgress` groups by `component` name, not index)
- Python API: zero references
- TypeScript frontend: zero references
- Go handler layer: zero references

`ProgressEvent.Total` is also duplicate — the same value is already delivered via `OnComponentTotal()`.

## Proposal

Remove:
- `pipeline.componentIndexMap()` method
- `ProgressEvent.Index` and `ProgressEvent.Total` fields

Keep `entity.ComponentIndex` DB column (avoid migration), stop assigning it.

## Related

- Branch: `feat/refactor-and-nats`
- File: `internal/ingestion/pipeline/pipeline.go:474-485`

title: [Bug]: Elasticsearch metadata date filtering — exact-match "=" returns 0 and LLM range operators are silently dropped
author: Xavierando
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues, including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report.
- [x] Non-english title submissions will be closed directly.
- [x] Please do not modify this template and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

5c8b51c (also reproducible on the v0.26.4 release)

### RAGFlow image version

v0.26.4

### Other environment information

- `DOC_ENGINE=elasticsearch`
- A document-metadata field whose values are ISO dates (`YYYY-MM-DD`), so ES dynamic date detection types it as `date`.

### Actual behavior

Three bugs break document-metadata **date** filtering on Elasticsearch. Bugs A and B affect both the chat `auto`/`semi_auto` metadata filter and `/api/v1/retrieval` with `metadata_condition`; bug C is latent on its own and is exposed once A and B are fixed.

**Bug A — exact-match `=` returns 0 on a `date`-typed field.**
`common/metadata_es_filter.py::_term_or_match` builds a `term` on `<field>.keyword` for string values. ES types a `YYYY-MM-DD` meta field as `date`, which has **no** `.keyword` sub-field, so the term matches nothing.
ES-level check: `term meta_fields.date = "2026-04-23"` → N hits, but `term meta_fields.date.keyword = "2026-04-23"` → 0.

**Bug B — ASCII comparison operators are silently dropped.**
`gen_meta_filter` (and users) may emit ASCII operators (`>=`, `<=`, `!=`, `==`), but `SUPPORTED_OPERATORS` and the in-memory `meta_filter` only accept the Unicode set (`≥`, `≤`, `≠`, `=`). An un-normalised operator makes the push-down raise `UnsupportedMetaFilter` (→ `None`) and the in-memory path match nothing, so the **whole filter is dropped** and retrieval is unrestricted.
Note: `convert_conditions()` already maps `{">=": "≥", ...}` for the `metadata_condition` input format, but this mapping is **not** applied to `gen_meta_filter` output or to manual conditions.

**Bug C — a filter that matches nothing falls back to *unrestricted* retrieval (latent; exposed by fixing A and B).**
`common/metadata_utils.py::apply_meta_data_filter` returns `None` from the `auto` and `semi_auto` branches when the generated conditions match zero documents. `None` means "no metadata filter", so retrieval runs **unrestricted** and returns arbitrary documents — the opposite of the intended "no results". The `manual` branch already handles this correctly by returning the `["-999"]` no-results sentinel.
This bug is pre-existing (the `return None` lines are unrelated to A/B), but it was **masked** by them: while `=`/range filtering was itself broken, a date filter never produced a *correct* empty match to trigger the fallback. With A and B fixed, a legitimately empty date query (e.g. "what happened yesterday", a date with no documents) now evaluates correctly to zero matches and hits this fallback — surfacing unrelated documents (often with a hallucinated date in the answer) instead of "no documents found".

### Expected behavior

`=` on a date meta field matches the documents with that date; `>=`/`<=`/… range conditions restrict correctly regardless of whether the operator arrives as ASCII or Unicode; and a filter that legitimately matches no document returns "no results" rather than falling back to unrestricted retrieval.

### Steps to reproduce

1. `DOC_ENGINE=elasticsearch`; a dataset with a `date` meta field (`YYYY-MM-DD` values).
2. A chat assistant with `meta_data_filter = {"method": "semi_auto", "semi_auto": ["date"]}` (or call `/api/v1/retrieval` with a `metadata_condition` using `=` / `>=`).
3. Ask a date-scoped question / send the filter.
4. Observe: `=` returns nothing (A); range conditions return everything unfiltered (B); and after fixing A and B, a date with no documents returns unrelated documents instead of "no results" (C).

Isolated clause-shape reproduction (no ES needed to see the produced clause):

```python
from common.metadata_es_filter import build_meta_filter_query, UnsupportedMetaFilter

# Bug A: '=' on a date value -> term on .keyword, which matches nothing on a `date` field
build_meta_filter_query([{"key": "date", "op": "=", "value": "2026-04-23"}], "and", ["<kb_id>"])

# Bug B: ASCII '>=' -> raises UnsupportedMetaFilter (condition silently dropped)
build_meta_filter_query([{"key": "date", "op": ">=", "value": "2026-04-01"}], "and", ["<kb_id>"])
```

### Additional information

Fix proposed in the accompanying PR: (A) route ISO-date values in `_term_or_match` to a `should` over both the parent field and `.keyword`; (B) add `normalize_condition_operators()` and apply it in `_run_metadata_filter`; (C) return the `["-999"]` no-results sentinel from the `auto`/`semi_auto` branches when a generated filter matches nothing (matching the existing `manual` behaviour). All three are covered by unit tests. Verified against a live Elasticsearch index: bug A `0 → 12` docs, bug B `dropped → 18` docs.

title: Go PUT /api/v1/documents/:id misses immutable-field validation that Python has (progress/run/chunk_num/token_num can be overwritten)
author: xugangqiang
body:
## Background

In Python, `PATCH /api/v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/documents/{document_id}` (implemented by `update_document` in `api/apps/restful_apis/document_api.py`) protects ingestion-managed document fields via a three-layer defense:

1. **`UpdateDocumentReq`** (in `api/utils/validation_utils.py:468`) accepts `progress`, `chunk_count`, `token_count` in the request schema — for backward compatibility with existing callers, not because they are writable.
2. **`validate_immutable_fields()`** (in `api/utils/validation_utils.py:1059`) rejects any non-zero change to these fields:
   - `progress`: rejected unless equal to DB value (within `math.isclose` tolerance)
   - `chunk_count`: rejected unless equal to `chunk_num` in DB
   - `token_count`: rejected unless equal to `token_num` in DB
3. **The handler body never writes these fields** — it only processes `name`, `parser_config`, `pipeline_id`, `chunk_method`, `enabled`, and `meta_fields`. The protected fields pass through validation and are silently dropped.

Python has no `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id` endpoint. The deprecated `PUT /datasets/{dataset_id}/documents/{document_id}` in `backward_compat.py:562` simply forwards to the PATCH handler.

## Gap in Go migration

Go introduced two document update endpoints with **inconsistent validation**:

| Endpoint | Go method | Validation |
|---|---|---|
| `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id` | `UpdateDocument` (`internal/service/document.go:598`) | **None** — loads the full entity via `GetByID`, overwrites any non-nil request field, then persists via `DB.Save` (GORM full-record upsert) |
| `PATCH /api/v1/datasets/:dataset_id/documents/:document_id` | `UpdateDatasetDocument` | ✅ Has `validateDatasetDocumentUpdate` (line 2640) — correctly prevents non-zero changes to `progress`, `chunk_count`, `token_count` |

The PUT endpoint's `UpdateDocument` unconditionally sets (lines 603–618):
```go
if req.Progress != nil    { document.Progress = *req.Progress }
if req.Run != nil         { document.Run = req.Run }
if req.ProgressMsg != nil { document.ProgressMsg = req.ProgressMsg }
if req.TokenNum != nil    { document.TokenNum = *req.TokenNum }
if req.ChunkNum != nil    { document.ChunkNum = *req.ChunkNum }
```

These are **ingestion-derived fields**, written by:
- `ProgressSink` (component progress aggregation → `document.progress` / `run` / `progress_msg`)
- `docStateUpdater` (post-pipeline metadata merge + `IncrementChunkNum`)

An external caller can `PUT {"progress": 0.5, "run": "0", "chunk_num": 0}` and the DB row is immediately overwritten. The ingestion pipeline's sink will later overwrite these values with the next component progress event, creating a transient inconsistency window.

## Impact

- **Data integrity**: Ingestion-managed fields (`progress`, `run`, `progress_msg`, `chunk_num`, `token_num`) can be arbitrarily set by any authenticated caller through the PUT endpoint.
- **Inconsistency with Python**: The same fields are correctly protected on the PATCH endpoint (matching Python's semantics), but the PUT endpoint has zero guarding.
- **Design inconsistency**: Two Go endpoints operating on the same document resource have divergent validation — PATCH has `validateDatasetDocumentUpdate`, PUT has nothing.

## Proposed fix

1. **Extract a shared validation function** `validateImmutableDocumentFields` in `internal/service/document.go` that checks `progress`/`chunk_num`/`token_num`/`run`/`progress_msg` against current DB values — matching Python's `validate_immutable_fields` semantics:
   - Zero is allowed (rerun reset)
   - Equal-to-current-value is allowed (idempotent write)
   - Any other change is rejected with a clear error message

2. **Call it in `UpdateDocument`** before the field-assignment block — the first thing after loading the entity from DB.

3. **Replace the three duplicate inline checks** in `validateDatasetDocumentUpdate` (lines 2644–2651) with a call to the shared function, removing duplication between the two endpoints.

4. **Remove the immutable fields from `UpdateDocument`'s write block** — they should be validated but never written (matching Python's handler semantics). Only `Name` remains as a truly user-editable field. The request struct keeps the fields (for future extension and backward compatibility), but they are read-only past the validation gate.

## Verification

- `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id {"progress": 0.5}` → 400 `"Can't change progress."`
- `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id {"progress": 0}` → 200 (rerun reset, allowed)
- `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id {"progress": 1.0}` when DB progress is 1.0 → 200 (idempotent, no-op)
- `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id {"run": "0"}` when DB run is "3" → 400 `"Can't change run."`
- `PUT /api/v1/documents/:id {"name": "new.pdf"}` → 200, only name changes
- `PATCH /api/v1/datasets/:id/documents/:id` behaves identically with the shared validation function (no behavioral change)
- Existing tests continue to pass: `internal/ingestion/service/execute_task_test.go` (checkpoint parse failure still marks task FAILED), `progress_sink_test.go` (sink still writes progress correctly via `UpdateRunProgress`), all ingestion and service pipeline tests.

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Go PDF parser places centered document title at the end of the section list (reading-order regression vs Python)
author: xugangqiang
body:
## Summary

The Go PDF parser (`internal/deepdoc/parser/pdf`) mis-orders a **centered document title**, placing it at the very end of `result.Sections` instead of at the top. The body text stays in correct top-to-bottom order; only the centered main title is displaced. This diverges from the Python reference parser and was caught by the integration test `TestIntegration_SectionsText`.

## Reproduction

Parse a single-column PDF whose document title is centered at the top of the page using the Go parser, then inspect `result.Sections`: the centered title appears as the **last** element rather than the first.

Detected via `TestIntegration_SectionsText` (`internal/deepdoc/parser/pdf/parser_pipeline_integration_test.go:97`), which compares section order against a golden snapshot.

## Actual vs expected

For a page with a centered title followed by left-aligned body paragraphs and left-aligned section headings:

```
expected: [Title, para1, para2, "Key Benefits", list, "Conclusion", finalPara]
actual:   [para1, para2, "Key Benefits", list, "Conclusion", finalPara, Title]
```

i.e. `actual == expected[1:] + expected[0:1]`.

Notably, the left-aligned section headings (`Key Benefits`, `Conclusion`) stay in their correct interleaved positions — **only the centered main title is displaced.**

## Root cause

Two factors combine.

1. **Column assignment treats the centered title as its own column.**
   `AssignColumn` (`internal/deepdoc/parser/pdf/layout/layout.go:22`) clusters boxes by `x0` per page. A centered title has an `x0` notably to the right of the left-aligned body, so KMeans + silhouette (`findBestK`, `layout.go:97`) selects `k=2`: the title gets `ColID=1`, the body `ColID=0`. Left-aligned headings share the body's `x0`, so they stay `ColID=0`.

2. **The final ordering is column-major, so `ColID=1` is emitted after all of `ColID=0`.**
   `buildLayout` (`internal/deepdoc/parser/pdf/parser.go:455`) calls `FinalReadingOrderMerge` (`parser.go:462`; sorts `page -> ColID -> top -> x0`) and then `NaiveVerticalMerge` -> `processPageBoxes` (`layout.go:364`), which buckets by `ColID` and emits columns in ascending `ColID` order. The centered title (`ColID=1`) is therefore emitted after every body box (`ColID=0`). Within `ColID=0`, boxes are still sorted by `top`, which is why the body remains correctly ordered and only the isolated title is pushed to the end.

## Divergence from the Python reference

The Go migration activated column-major ordering that the Python production path does **not** use.

Python production path: `rag/flow/parser/parser.py` -> `parse_into_bboxes` -> `_parse_loaded_window_into_bboxes` (`deepdoc/parser/pdf_parser.py:1741`):

```
_layouts_rec
_table_transformer_job
_text_merge              # col_id used ONLY to block cross-column horizontal merge
_concat_downward         # -> Recognizer.sort_Y_firstly(self.boxes, 0)  (pure top sort)
_naive_vertical_merge    # groups by page only; col_id grouping is COMMENTED OUT
_extract_table_figure    # spatial nearest-neighbor insertion (min_rectangle_distance)
```

- `sort_Y_firstly` (`deepdoc/vision/recognizer.py:54`) with `threshold=0` is a pure sort by `top` -> final order is `page -> top -> x0`. No `col_id` in the reading order.
- `_naive_vertical_merge` (`pdf_parser.py:928`) has its `col_id` grouping commented out (line 934) and the `col_id`-keyed final sort commented out (line 1006); it groups by page and sorts by `(top, x0)`.
- `_final_reading_order_merge` (`pdf_parser.py:1009`) — the column-major sort that mirrors Go's `FinalReadingOrderMerge` — is **dead code**: defined but never called.
- `col_id` is used **only** in `_text_merge` (`pdf_parser.py:909`) to avoid merging boxes across columns horizontally.

## Regression source

PR #16795 (merge commit `12787996d`, 2026-07-10, "DeepDOC parser hardening") rewrote `processPageBoxes` from a global `(Top, X0)` sort into `ColID`-bucketed column-major emission. The previous global-`Top` sort matched Python's `_naive_vertical_merge`; the new version reintroduces the column-major approach Python abandoned. (Go calling `FinalReadingOrderMerge` at all is a separate, older divergence — it corresponds to Python's dead `_final_reading_order_merge`.)

## Suggested fix

Align Go's final ordering with Python's production path (`page -> top -> x0`) and restrict `col_id` to horizontal-merge gating:

1. `processPageBoxes` (`layout.go:364`): revert to grouping by page and sorting by `(top, x0)` within the page — i.e. drop the `ColID` bucketing. This restores pre-#16795 behavior and matches Python's `_naive_vertical_merge`.
2. `FinalReadingOrderMerge` (`layout.go:249`): drop the `ColID` key (sort `page -> top -> x0`), or stop calling it — mirroring Python, which never calls `_final_reading_order_merge`.
3. Keep `col_id` in `TextMerge` to prevent cross-column horizontal merges (matches Python `_text_merge`).
4. (Optional, for full parity) Insert tables/figures via spatial nearest-neighbor (`min_rectangle_distance`) as Python does, instead of relying on column order.

This preserves correct column handling where it legitimately matters (horizontal-merge boundaries) while restoring top-to-bottom reading order for single-column documents with centered titles. Regenerating the golden snapshot is **not** the right fix — the new order is semantically wrong.

## Environment

- Regression introduced: PR #16795 (merge commit `12787996d`, 2026-07-10)
- Affected files:
  - `internal/deepdoc/parser/pdf/layout/layout.go` (`processPageBoxes`, `FinalReadingOrderMerge`, `AssignColumn`)
  - `internal/deepdoc/parser/pdf/parser.go` (`buildLayout`)
- Python reference: `deepdoc/parser/pdf_parser.py`, `deepdoc/vision/recognizer.py`

Line numbers are as of the current `main`.

title: [Bug]: TokenChunker ignores TitleChunker's chunks output and reads the parser's raw json field instead when output_format="chunks"
author: dc277703639
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues), including closed ones.
- [x] I confirm that I am using English to submit this report ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Non-english title submitions will be closed directly ( 非英文标题的提交将会被直接关闭 ) ([Language Policy](https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/5910)).
- [x] Please do not modify this template :) and fill in all the required fields.

### RAGFlow workspace code commit ID

26d3480e

### RAGFlow image version

26d3480e(0.26.4)

### Other environment information

```Markdown
OS: Windows
Browser: Edge 
Pipeline: Parser → TitleChunker(hierarchy) → TokenChunker →tokenizer
```

### Actual behavior

When chaining **TitleChunker → TokenChunker** in an Agent/Canvas workflow, **TokenChunker does NOT consume the chapter-level chunks produced by TitleChunker**. Instead, it reads the **parser's original raw `json` field** (flat line-level entries with no chapter hierarchy), completely discarding TitleChunker's output.

This causes cascading failures downstream:

1. **PageIndex (TOC) extraction** produces incorrect/duplicate/missing chapters — because it receives flat line-level text instead of properly structured chapter blocks.
2. **Retrieval quality degrades** — chunks are no longer aligned with document structure; cross-chapter merging occurs silently.

### Root cause (source-code verified)

**TitleChunker** (`rag/flow/chunker/title_chunker/title_chunker.py`) sets:
```python
self.set_output("output_format", "chunks")   # outputs "chunks"
self.set_chunks(chunks)                       # writes chapter-aware chunks here
```
It does **not** touch the `json` field — that field is the parser's original output, passed through untouched.

**TokenChunker** (`rag/flow/chunker/token_chunker.py`, `_invoke` around line 314):
```python
if from_upstream.output_format in ["markdown", "text", "html"]:
    # ... text processing path ...
# Falls through to HERE because "chunks" is NOT in ["markdown","text","html"]
json_result = from_upstream.json_result or []   # Reads PARSER'S RAW json!
```

Key evidence table:

| Fact | Source Location |
|------|-----------------|
| No branch for `output_format == "chunks"` exists | `token_chunker.py` L314 |
| `from_upstream.chunks` defined in schema but never referenced by `_invoke` | `schema.py` L26; entire `token_chunker.py _invoke` |
| The `json` field = parser's flat line-level segmentation | Debug dump `_parse_5.json` |
| TitleChunker only writes to `chunks`, never touches `json` | `title_chunker/common.py` L65, L282–290 |

### Data evidence from debug dump

A JSON dump after TitleChunker execution shows **three top-level fields**:

| Field | Content | Source |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `chunks` | Chapter-level blocks; each `text` prefixed with full ancestor-title path (e.g., `"第一章 总则\n第一条 ..."`) | **TitleChunker — correct data** |
| `json` | Flat entries, one per line/paragraph; NO chapter hierarchy | **Parser original — wrong for downstream** |
| `output_format` | `"chunks"` | Set by TitleChunker |

TokenChunker reads `json` instead of `chunks`.

### Impact scope

This affects **any pipeline where TitleChunker (or any chunker setting `output_format="chunks"`) feeds into TokenChunker**:

- **PageIndex (TOC)**: wrong/repeated/missing chapter titles
- **Retrieval**: chunks misaligned with document structure
- **Any Extractor node**: operates on malformed input

### Expected behavior

When `from_upstream.output_format == "chunks"` and `from_upstream.chunks` is non-empty, TokenChunker should:

1. Read `from_upstream.chunks` (the chapter-aware output from upstream chunkers like TitleChunker).
2. Process each chunk as a parent unit — applying token-based splitting **within** each chapter block without merging across chapter boundaries.
3. Preserve structural metadata so downstream components (PageIndex, retrieval) can leverage chapter information.

In short: the `chunks` field produced by upstream chunkers should be respected as the source of truth, and the parser's raw `json` field should NOT be used when proper chunked data is available.

### Steps to reproduce

```Markdown
1. Open RAGFlow Agent/Canvas editor.
2. Create a pipeline: **Parser → TitleChunker (method=hierarchy) → TokenChunker → Extractor (result=PageIndex)**.
3. Upload a structured document with clear chapter headings (e.g., any regulation/document with `第X章` / `Chapter N` headings).
4. Run the pipeline.
5. Inspect the intermediate output between TitleChunker and TokenChunker, or examine the final PageIndex TOC result.

**Observed result**: PageIndex TOC shows duplicate titles, missing entire chapters, broken hierarchy levels, and chunk content that does not correspond to chapter boundaries.

**Expected result**: TokenChunker should process TitleChunker's chapter-structured chunks, preserving chapter boundaries. Downstream PageIndex should produce correct TOC with proper hierarchy.
```

### Additional information

### Suggested fix

In `rag/flow/chunker/token_chunker.py`, add a branch for `output_format == "chunks"` **before** the existing JSON fallback (around line 314):

```python
# --- NEW: handle upstream chapter-aware chunks (e.g. from TitleChunker) ---
if from_upstream.output_format == "chunks" and getattr(from_upstream, 'chunks', None):
    chunks = _build_json_chunks(from_upstream.chunks, delimiter_pattern)
    _attach_context_to_media_chunks(chunks, self._param.table_context_size,
                                     self._param.image_context_size)
    if custom_pattern:
        chunks = _split_chunk_docs_by_children(chunks, custom_pattern)
    await restore_pdf_text_previews(chunks, from_upstream, self._canvas)
    cks = _finalize_json_chunks(chunks)
    self.set_output("chunks", cks)
    self.callback(1, "Done.")
    return
# --- END NEW ---

# Original JSON fallback (unchanged for other output_format values)
json_result = from_upstream.json_result or []
```

This ensures:
- When upstream provides properly chunked data (`output_format=="chunks"`), it is used as the source of truth.
- Chapter boundaries are preserved because `_build_json_chunks` treats each upstream chunk as an atomic unit.
- Existing behavior for all other `output_format` values remains unchanged.

### Workaround (no code change required)

Use **only one Chunker** in your pipeline — either TitleChunker **or** TokenChunker, but not both chained together. If you need title-based chunking, use TitleChunker alone (its output is already final). If you need token-based chunking, use TokenChunker directly after the Parser.

Alternatively, reverse the order: **Parser → TokenChunker → TitleChunker**. TitleChunker correctly handles `output_format=="chunks"` input (see `rag/flow/chunker/title_chunker/common.py` line 111), so placing it last produces correct chapter-aware output.

title: [Bug]: QA parser for .csv silently corrupts Q&A pairs: csv.reader quote handling + row/line index mismatch splice answers from wrong lines
author: GitHhb
body:
### Self Checks

- I have searched for existing issues (including closed ones). #16433 touched this code
  but only fixed the row number of the final pair; the defects below are still present
  on current `main`.

### RAGFlow version

Observed on a v0.26.x deployment; the affected code is still present on current `main`
(`rag/app/qa.py`, csv branch, ~lines 347–375).

### Actual behavior

When parsing a TAB-delimited `.csv` with the **Q&A chunk method**, chunks silently get
content from the *wrong* lines of the file:

- answers absorb raw rows of *other* Q&A pairs (including the TAB and the other pair's
  question),
- some continuation lines disappear entirely,
- some Q&A pairs vanish without any failure being reported.

On a real 679k-line / 13,435-pair file (Dutch helpdesk tickets, where memo lines
regularly start with a `"` character), this produced 13,412 chunks of which **7,902
contained the content of more than one ticket**, and 10 questions were missing
altogether — with `progress_msg` reporting success.

### Expected behavior

Each `question<TAB>answer` row becomes exactly one chunk; continuation lines (no TAB)
are appended to the answer of the *current* pair, as documented.

### Steps to reproduce

The csv branch does this (simplified from `rag/app/qa.py`):

```python
lines = txt.split("\n")
reader = csv.reader(lines, delimiter=delimiter)
for i, row in enumerate(reader):
    if len(row) != 2:
        if question:
            answer += "\n" + lines[i]     # <-- BUG 2
    elif len(row) == 2:
        ...
        question, answer = row
```

Two defects interact:

1. **Unwanted quote handling.** `csv.reader` is used with the default
   `quotechar='"'`. Any continuation line that *starts* with `"` and does not close
   the quote on the same line opens a quoted field that swallows the following
   physical lines (including whole `Q<TAB>A` rows) into a single reader record.
2. **Row index used as line index.** In the `len(row) != 2` fallback, the reader's
   *record* index `i` is used to index the *physical* `lines` list. After any
   multi-line quoted record, `i` permanently lags the physical line number, so every
   subsequent fallback appends text from the wrong line — this is how other tickets'
   rows end up inside unrelated answers. The lag grows with every quote-open line.

Minimal reproduction (pure Python, mirrors the loop above):

```python
import csv

lines = [
    'Q1: how to reset password\tA1 step one',
    '"see the attached error log',              # opens a quote, does not close it
    'error: disk full"',                        # closes the quote
    'Q2: printer offline\tA2 restart spooler',
    'done',
    'Q3: vpn drops\tA3 update client',
    'reboot if needed',
]

reader = csv.reader(lines, delimiter="\t")
question, answer, res = "", "", []
for i, row in enumerate(reader):
    if len(row) != 2:
        if question:
            answer += "\n" + lines[i]
    else:
        if question and answer:
            res.append((question, answer))
        question, answer = row
if question:
    res.append((question, answer))

for q, a in res:
    print(repr(q), "->", repr(a))
```

Actual output:

```
'Q1: how to reset password' -> 'A1 step one\n"see the attached error log'
'Q2: printer offline' -> 'A2 restart spooler\nQ2: printer offline\tA2 restart spooler'
'Q3: vpn drops' -> 'A3 update client\nQ3: vpn drops\tA3 update client'
```

- Q1 lost the line `error: disk full"`.
- Q2 and Q3 never receive their real continuation lines (`done`, `reboot if needed`);
  instead their own raw row text (TAB included) is spliced into their answers. In a
  large file the growing offset splices *other* pairs' rows instead.

Expected output:

```
'Q1: how to reset password' -> 'A1 step one\n"see the attached error log\nerror: disk full"'
'Q2: printer offline' -> 'A2 restart spooler\ndone'
'Q3: vpn drops' -> 'A3 update client\nreboot if needed'
```

### Suggested fix

The QA csv format is documented as "UTF-8, TAB as delimiter"; quote characters have no
documented meaning, and free-text answers legitimately contain them. Two options:

1. `csv.reader(lines, delimiter=delimiter, quoting=csv.QUOTE_NONE)` — every physical
   line is then exactly one record, which fixes both defects at once (`i` stays in
   sync with `lines`), or
2. keep quote handling but stop indexing `lines[i]`: reconstruct the raw line from the
   parsed record (`delimiter.join(row)`), or track the physical position via
   `reader.line_num`.

Option 1 is the least invasive and matches the documented format. Happy to submit a PR
for either direction, including a regression test based on the reproduction above.

### Additional information

The `.txt` branch has a related weakness: the comma/TAB detection counts lines that
split into exactly two parts, so natural-language answers full of salutations like
"regards, John" can outvote the actual TAB rows and flip the delimiter to comma for
the whole file. A shared fix could prefer TAB whenever any TAB-row is present.

title: [Bug]: Connector logs API fails on PostgreSQL due to SELECT DISTINCT ordering by non-selected update_time
author: emonupg
body:
### Self Checks

- [x] I have searched for existing issues and did not find an exact duplicate.
- [x] I have confirmed this happens with PostgreSQL.

### RAGFlow Version

Current Docker image / current upstream Python backend behavior. I also checked current `main` source shape while preparing this report.

### What happened?

Calling the connector sync logs API fails when RAGFlow is configured with PostgreSQL:

```http
GET /api/v1/connectors/{connector_id}/logs
```

The request reaches:

```text
api/apps/restful_apis/connector_api.py:list_logs
api/db/services/connector_service.py:SyncLogsService.list_sync_tasks
```

and then crashes with:

```text
peewee.ProgrammingError: for SELECT DISTINCT, ORDER BY expressions must appear in select list
```

Full PostgreSQL error excerpt:

```text
psycopg2.errors.InvalidColumnReference: for SELECT DISTINCT, ORDER BY expressions must appear in select list
LINE 1: ... ORDER BY "t1"."upda...
```

### Root cause

In `api/db/services/connector_service.py`, `SyncLogsService.list_sync_tasks()` builds a select list that does not include `SyncLogs.update_time`:

```python
fields = [
    cls.model.id,
    cls.model.connector_id,
    cls.model.task_type,
    cls.model.kb_id,
    cls.model.update_date,
    cls.model.new_docs_indexed,
    cls.model.total_docs_indexed,
    cls.model.docs_removed_from_index,
    cls.model.error_msg,
    cls.model.error_count,
    cls.model.time_started.alias("time_started"),
    Connector.refresh_freq.alias("refresh_freq"),
    Connector.prune_freq.alias("prune_freq"),
    Knowledgebase.name.alias("kb_name"),
    cls.model.status,
]
```

but later always applies:

```python
query = query.distinct().order_by(cls.model.update_time.desc())
```

PostgreSQL requires any `ORDER BY` expression used with `SELECT DISTINCT` to appear in the selected columns, so this query fails.

There is a related due-task query path later in the same file that already includes `cls.model.update_time` in its selected fields before ordering by it, so the logs API path appears inconsistent.

### Expected behavior

`GET /api/v1/connectors/{connector_id}/logs` should return connector sync logs normally on PostgreSQL.

### Actual behavior

The API returns an internal server error and logs:

```text
peewee.ProgrammingError: for SELECT DISTINCT, ORDER BY expressions must appear in select list
```

### Reproduction

1. Run RAGFlow with `DB_TYPE=postgres`.
2. Create or use a data source connector.
3. Trigger or schedule at least one sync so `sync_logs` has records.
4. Request:

```http
GET /api/v1/connectors/{connector_id}/logs?page=1&page_size=15
```

5. Observe the PostgreSQL `InvalidColumnReference` error.

### Suggested fix

Include `cls.model.update_time` in the selected fields for `SyncLogsService.list_sync_tasks()` when ordering by it, remove `distinct()` if it is not required, or change the query to a PostgreSQL-safe distinct strategy.

A minimal candidate fix may be:

```python
fields = [
    ...
    cls.model.status,
    cls.model.update_time,
]
```

### Additional context

Related PostgreSQL issue: https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow/issues/11293

That issue appears to focus on PostgreSQL startup / scheduled sync SQL compatibility. This report is specifically for the connector logs endpoint:

```text
/api/v1/connectors/{connector_id}/logs
```

title: [Bug]: The vulnerability I submitted according to your security policy has not received a response for a long time
author: August829
body:
### RAGFlow image version

0.24.0

```Markdown
I discovered and submitted four vulnerabilities according to your security policy several months ago, but I have not received any response.
```

title: tests: centralize Go tokenizer test resource initialization and stop using Python .venv resources
author: xugangqiang
body:
## Summary

Our Go integration tests currently initialize tokenizer resources in multiple places, and some helpers assemble a synthetic resource directory from Python `.venv` contents. This can poison the global Go tokenizer pool and make later tests fail in misleading ways.

## Observed failure mode

In `internal/ingestion/pipeline` integration tests:

- `TestPipelineRun_TemplateGeneral_RealMySQLMinIO_OutputShape` runs first and initializes the global tokenizer pool.
- A later test such as `TestPipelineRun_TemplateGeneral_RealComponents` then fails with missing `content_ltks`.
- `search_method` is still `["embedding", "full_text"]`, so the tokenizer params are correct.
- The actual issue is that `tokenizer.Tokenize("Alpha paragraph.")` returns an empty string after the earlier helper has initialized the pool from synthetic resources.
- As a result, `content_ltks` becomes an empty string and disappears from JSON output because of `omitempty`.

This is not a pipeline/dataflow logic bug. It is a tokenizer test resource initialization bug.

## Root cause

Some integration helpers build `RAGFLOW_DICT_PATH` from Python `.venv` files and synthetic directories instead of using a canonical tokenizer resource root.

That approach is fragile because:

- `.venv` is not a stable Go runtime contract.
- The Go/C++ tokenizer expects a complete resource root layout.
- Tests initialize a global singleton pool, so one bad initialization affects later tests in the same package.

## Proposed cleanup

1. Add a unified tokenizer resource resolver.
   - Put it under `internal/ingestion/testutil` or `internal/tokenizer`.
   - Validate that a resource root is complete before using it.

2. Add a unified tokenizer test init helper.
   - Centralize `Skip` vs `Fail` behavior.
   - Centralize `tokenizer.Init(...)` calls.

3. Remove `.venv`-based resource assembly from tests.
   - Remove `prepareTokenizerResourceForIntegration` synthetic assembly logic.
   - Remove `prepareTokenizerResourceForTaskIntegration` synthetic assembly logic.
   - Remove related symlink / generated `pos-id.def` / `wordnet` / `opencc` assembly helpers.

4. Replace call sites.
   - `internal/ingestion/pipeline` integration tests.
   - `internal/ingestion/task` integration tests.
   - Any other test helpers that manipulate `RAGFLOW_DICT_PATH` directly.

5. Add focused tests.
   - Resolver behavior.
   - Helper behavior.
   - Invalid configured path should fail loudly.
   - Missing environment / missing canonical path should skip integration tests.

6. Regression verification.
   - Re-run the minimal reproducer:
     - `TestPipelineRun_TemplateGeneral_RealMySQLMinIO_OutputShape`
     - `TestPipelineRun_TemplateGeneral_RealComponents`
   - Re-run the full integration test packages:
     - `internal/ingestion/pipeline`
     - `internal/ingestion/task`

## Recommended resource selection policy

For tests that need a real tokenizer resource root:

1. If `RAGFLOW_DICT_PATH` is already set:
   - validate it
   - use it
   - fail if it is invalid

2. Otherwise, if `/usr/share/infinity/resource` exists and is valid:
   - use it

3. Otherwise:
   - skip the integration test

This keeps Go tokenizer tests aligned with the actual runtime contract and avoids depending on Python packaging internals.
